text.title: Agent Drift Detection (Statistical) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of agent-drift-detection-statistical enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Pass-rate control chart uses CUSUM with k=0.5 and h=4 (standard EWMA defaults); alert when CUSUM exceeds h.
rule.rationale: Single-day pass-rate drops are noisy; CUSUM accumulates small drifts that single-threshold detectors miss.

rule.statement: Output-distribution drift is measured by KL divergence over a categorical bucketing of the output (intent, schema-key set, length bucket). Alert at KL ≥ 0.1 vs baseline.
rule.rationale: KL surfaces distribution shifts before any single metric crosses a threshold.

rule.statement: Judge-score regression uses a paired t-test against baseline; significance threshold p<0.05 with Bonferroni correction across metrics.
rule.rationale: Without correction, the 'false alarm' rate compounds across N metrics; Bonferroni keeps family-wise error bounded.

rule.statement: Drift alert severity: yellow (KL ≥ 0.05 OR CUSUM ≥ 2), red (KL ≥ 0.1 OR CUSUM ≥ 4 OR p<0.01 regression).
rule.rationale: Severity bands route to different responses; binary alerts produce alarm fatigue.

rule.statement: Every alert triggers a retest with a 2x sample size; if retest is also red, freeze deploys; if yellow, course-correct without freeze.
rule.rationale: Single-run alerts are noisy; double-sample retest cuts false positives ~50%.

rule.statement: Stub rule for conclusion 'skip-methodology' referenced from 06-decision-tree.xml. Replace with the real testable rule that resolves this branch of the tree.
rule.rationale: Auto-added by fix-methodology-phase-d.py to keep tree refs resolvable.
